decision_policy.claim_boundary: This artifact freezes a candidate direction and null competitors. It does not provide a Tau-specific time-shift detection.
minimum_before_fit: define one-amplitude T2 operator on image-level features
preferably confirm the design vector on an independent lens target

purpose: Pre-register the residual direction and null competitors for a later bounded DES J0408 T2 comparison without fitting or sampling T2.

rows.description: A sufficiently flexible no-T2 lens/source/nuisance model explains the DES residual without a T2 correction.
rows.success_condition: Residual projection along the frozen design vector is consistent with zero after the no-T2 baseline and nuisance policy are fixed.

rows.description: A one-amplitude T2 correction is tested only along the frozen observed-minus-model residual direction.
rows.success_condition: A shared-amplitude correction improves the held-out likelihood or predefined residual score against all listed null controls.

rows.description: The residual direction is an ordinary lens-family or source-model mismatch, not a time-distortion operator.
rows.success_condition: Alternative no-T2 lens/source families reduce the frozen residual as well as or better than the T2 candidate.

rows.description: The residual direction is induced by public-row linkage, legacy runtime semantics, or DES-specific posterior handling.
rows.success_condition: The effect disappears or materially changes after independent row recovery, legacy-runtime reconstruction, or a second target.

rows.description: The non-clean DES feature rows show a similar residual direction, so coherence alone is not a T2-specific discriminator.
rows.success_condition: A future T2 claim must pass a stronger holdout or independent lens test; coherence alone cannot be counted as evidence.

schema: paper7 DES J0408 null-vs-T2 comparison design freeze v1
